parameters.content:
## SaaS Subscription Waste Auditor — Gmail + GPT-4.1-mini + Google Sheets + HTML Email Report

For teams, agencies, and individuals who want to automatically audit all SaaS subscription charges in their Gmail inbox, classify waste, and receive a weekly HTML digest showing exactly how much money can be recovered. This workflow scans Gmail for billing and subscription emails from the last 30 days using a broad keyword search. A Code node prefilters marketing noise — keeping only invoices, receipts, renewals, and billing confirmations. For each email the full body is fetched. An AI Agent powered by GPT-4.1-mini runs a 5-step analysis: classify billing vs non-billing, extract 18 structured fields (vendor, amount, cycle, renewal date, waste type), optionally call Gmail search and Get Message tools to confirm amounts and check for usage signals, classify waste type (OK / UNUSED / ZOMBIE / ANNUAL_RENEWAL / RENEWAL_SOON), and compute confidence score with routing (_target: dashboard / review / skip). A Code node validates and cleans the JSON. Google Sheets upserts one row per vendor (Vendor as match key). A Code node builds a branded HTML report with stat boxes, flagged subscription table, and cost summary. Gmail sends the report.

This workflow also includes an embedded sub-workflow (bottom) that handles Gmail search tool calls invoked by the AI Agent.

## How it works
- **1. Schedule — Monthly Billing Scan** triggers the pipeline (currently set to monthly — edit cron to run weekly)
- **2. Gmail — Search Billing and Usage Emails** searches last 30 days for billing, invoice, subscription, and re-engagement keyword matches — limit 500
- **3. Code — Prefilter Noise** drops newsletters, promos, OTPs — keeps only real billing signals
- **4. Gmail — Get Email Body** fetches the full email body for each filtered message
- **5. AI Agent — Extract and Analyze Subscription** runs 5-step analysis using GPT-4.1-mini with two ai_tools: Get a message in Gmail (confirm amounts) and Call Gmail Search (check for usage signals via sub-workflow)
- **6. Code — Parse and Validate** strips markdown from AI output, validates JSON, recalculates Est. Annual Cost, validates date format, routes low-confidence items to review
- **7. Google Sheets — Upsert Dashboard** upserts 18 columns to the Dashboard sheet matching on Vendor — updates existing or adds new rows
- **8. Code — Build HTML Report** aggregates all rows, computes waste totals, builds a branded HTML email with stat boxes and flagged subscription table
- **9. Gmail — Send Weekly Report** sends the HTML digest to your email address

## Sub-workflow (bottom of canvas)
The sub-workflow receives Gmail search queries from the AI Agent tool call, executes them via Gmail, and returns results. Replace `YOUR_SUBWORKFLOW_ID` in the Call Gmail Search tool node.

## Set up steps
1. In **2, 4, 9** — connect your Gmail OAuth2 credential and replace credential IDs
2. In **OpenAI — GPT-4.1-mini Model** — connect your OpenAI API credential
3. In **7. Google Sheets — Upsert Dashboard** — connect Google Sheets OAuth2 credential and replace `YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID`. Create a Dashboard sheet tab with columns: Vendor, Category, Amount, Currency, Billing Cycle, Monthly Equivalent, Renewal Date, Days to Renewal, Status, Waste Type, Est. Annual Cost, Priority, Recommended Action, Confidence, First Seen, Last Updated, Source Email
4. In **9. Gmail — Send Weekly Report** — replace `REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_EMAIL@example.com`
5. In **Call Gmail Search** tool node — replace `YOUR_SUBWORKFLOW_ID` with the ID of the sub-workflow (copy from browser URL when viewing the sub-workflow)
6. In the sub-workflow Gmail node — connect the same Gmail credential

nodes.name: Section — Schedule, Gmail Search, Noise Filter, and Body Fetch

parameters.content:
## Schedule, Gmail Search, Noise Filter, and Body Fetch
Schedule triggers the scan. Gmail searches last 30 days for billing and subscription emails. Code prefilters marketing noise — keeps only real billing signals. Gmail fetches the full body for each filtered message.

nodes.name: Section — AI Agent 5-Step Subscription Extraction and Waste Analysis

parameters.content:
## AI Agent — 5-Step Subscription Extraction and Waste Analysis
GPT-4.1-mini runs: classify billing vs non-billing, extract 18 structured fields, optionally call Gmail tools to confirm amounts and check usage signals, classify waste type, compute confidence and route to dashboard or review. Two ai_tools: Get a message in Gmail and Call Gmail Search sub-workflow.

nodes.name: Section — JSON Parse, Validate, and Sheets Upsert

parameters.content:
## JSON Parse, Validate, and Sheets Upsert
Strips markdown from AI output, validates JSON, recalculates annual cost, validates date format, routes low-confidence items. Upserts to Google Sheets Dashboard matching on Vendor key.

nodes.name: Section — HTML Report Build and Gmail Send

parameters.content:
## HTML Report Build and Gmail Send
Aggregates all upserted rows, computes waste totals, builds branded HTML email with stat boxes and flagged subscription table sorted by annual cost. Gmail sends the digest.

## Gmail Search Sub-Workflow
This sub-workflow is called by the AI Agent tool when it needs to search Gmail for usage signals or confirm billing details. The trigger receives the search query from the parent workflow and returns Gmail results.

nodes.name: 2. Gmail — Search Billing and Usage Emails

filters.q: newer_than:30d (subject:(invoice OR receipt OR subscription OR renewal OR payment OR billing) OR from:(billing OR noreply OR no-reply OR invoice OR receipts) OR subject:("we miss you" OR "haven't seen you" OR "come back" OR reactivate OR "your account is inactive"))

nodes.name: 5. AI Agent — Extract and Analyze Subscription

parameters.text:
=Today is {{ $now.toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }}.
You are a subscription billing analyst. Your job: analyze the email below, confirm it using Gmail search if needed, then return ONE raw JSON object that maps directly to a Google Sheet row.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════
PRIMARY EMAIL
══════════════════════════════════════════════════
FROM    : {{ $json.From || $json.from || '' }}
SUBJECT : {{ $json.Subject || $json.subject || '' }}
DATE    : {{ $json.date || $json.internalDate || '' }}
BODY    : {{ String($json.text || $json.snippet || '').slice(0, 3500) }}

══════════════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 1 — QUICK CLASSIFY
══════════════════════════════════════════════════
Is this a real recurring paid charge (invoice, receipt, renewal notice, subscription confirmation, failed payment, trial-ending charge warning)?

Non-billing = newsletters, marketing, "X% off" promos, OTPs, social notifications, password resets.
If clearly non-billing → go directly to OUTPUT with is_billing=false. Skip all other steps.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 2 — EXTRACT CORE FIELDS
══════════════════════════════════════════════════
From the primary email extract:
- vendor      — clean company/product name (not email domain)
- category    — one of: CRM, Design, Dev Tools, Marketing, Storage, Email, Hosting, Analytics, Project Mgmt, Finance, HR, Security, Communication, Other
- amount      — numeric only, no currency symbols. null if not found.
- currency    — ISO code: USD, EUR, GBP, INR, etc. Default USD if unclear.
- billing_cycle — monthly | annual | quarterly | weekly | one-time | unknown
- renewal_date — YYYY-MM-DD from any mention of "next billing", "renews on", "charged on", "valid until". null if not found.

Compute:
- monthly_equivalent:
    annual    → amount ÷ 12
    quarterly → amount ÷ 3
    weekly    → amount × 4.33
    monthly / one-time → amount as-is
    null amount → 0
- days_to_renewal — whole integer days from today ({{ $now.toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }}) to renewal_date. null if renewal_date is null.
- est_annual_cost = monthly_equivalent × 12

══════════════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 3 — CONFIRM WITH GMAIL TOOLS (do this when unsure)
══════════════════════════════════════════════════
Use tools when:
a) Amount is unclear → search "from:[sender domain] invoice receipt"
b) Renewal date is missing → search "from:[sender domain] subscription renews"
c) You want to check if this tool is being actively used → search "from:[sender domain] newer_than:90d" — if only billing emails show up and NO login/activity/usage emails exist, mark as potential ZOMBIE
d) Usage-signal check → search "from:[sender domain] (we miss you OR haven't seen you OR come back OR your account is inactive OR reactivate)" — if found, mark as UNUSED

After each search, use "Get a message in Gmail" on the 1-2 most relevant message IDs to read full body for date/amount confirmation.
Maximum 4 tool calls total — be efficient.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 4 — WASTE TYPE + PRIORITY
══════════════════════════════════════════════════
Apply the FIRST matching rule (multiple can apply — use the most severe):

| Rule            | Condition                                                        | waste_type       | Priority |
|-----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|----------|
| Unused          | Step 3 found re-engagement / "we miss you" email from vendor     | UNUSED           | High     |
| Zombie          | Step 3: only billing emails in 90d, zero activity emails found   | ZOMBIE           | High     |
| Annual Renewal  | billing_cycle=annual AND days_to_renewal is 0-30                 | ANNUAL_RENEWAL   | High     |
| Renewal Soon    | days_to_renewal is 0-30 (any cycle)                              | RENEWAL_SOON     | Medium (High if annual) |
| Healthy         | None of the above                                                | OK               | Low      |

recommended_action — one short sentence per type:
- UNUSED         → "Cancel — no recent activity detected"
- ZOMBIE         → "Verify usage — no non-billing emails in 90 days"
- ANNUAL_RENEWAL → "Review before [renewal_date] — annual charge incoming"
- RENEWAL_SOON   → "Review before [renewal_date] — renews soon"
- OK             → "Active subscription — monitor next cycle"

══════════════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 5 — CONFIDENCE + ROUTING
══════════════════════════════════════════════════
Start with AI's own confidence (0.0-1.0).
Then reduce:
- amount is null     → -0.15
- renewal_date null  → -0.10
- vendor unclear     → -0.15
- parse from snippet only (no full body) → -0.10

_target logic:
- is_billing=true  AND confidence >= 0.6  → "_target": "dashboard"
- is_billing=true  AND confidence < 0.6  → "_target": "review"
- is_billing=false                       → "_target": "skip"

══════════════════════════════════════════════════
OUTPUT — STRICT RULES
══════════════════════════════════════════════════
- Return ONLY a raw JSON object — zero markdown, zero backticks, zero explanation
- JSON keys must EXACTLY match the names below (spaces, capitalization identical)
- Never invent amount or date — use null
- All number fields must be numbers, not strings

{
  "is_billing": true,
  "Vendor": "string",
  "Category": "string",
  "Amount": 0.00,
  "Currency": "USD",
  "Billing Cycle": "monthly | annual | quarterly | weekly | one-time | unknown",
  "Monthly Equivalent": 0.00,
  "Renewal Date": "YYYY-MM-DD or null",
  "Days to Renewal": 0,
  "Status": "new",
  "Waste Type": "OK | UNUSED | ZOMBIE | ANNUAL_RENEWAL | RENEWAL_SOON",
  "Est. Annual Cost": 0.00,
  "Priority": "High | Medium | Low",
  "Recommended Action": "short action string",
  "Confidence": 0.00,
  "First Seen": "{{ $now.toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }}",
  "Last Updated": "{{ $now.toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }}",
  "Source Email": "{{ $json.From || $json.from || '' }}",
  "_target": "dashboard | review | skip"
}
